text:
You are building an admin product catalog page for an e-commerce team.

The catalog is read-heavy and should feel fast for day-to-day browsing, so avoid re-querying product data on every request.

Admins can trigger a "Sync latest catalog" action from the page when upstream ERP/PIM data changes (pricing, inventory, availability). After submitting, they should be able to continue working immediately, even if the product list is briefly stale.

The expected behavior is that product data is refreshed in the background and becomes up to date shortly after, across any views that depend on the same catalog data.

Implement the sync trigger as a regular HTML form that uses an inline Server Action in the page.
Use the cache tag name "products" consistently for catalog caching and invalidation.

In practice, sync jobs can touch thousands of SKUs, so operators prioritize a responsive admin experience and eventual consistency across catalog views over forcing every request to block on freshly recomputed data.

I’m going to inspect the app structure to find the catalog page, the data-fetching path, and the current caching strategy so I can wire the sync form into the right server action and invalidate the shared `products` tag without making requests block on recomputation.

I’ve confirmed this is a very small Next app, so I’m reading the entry page and config now to see whether the catalog is already present or if I need to build the page and data cache path from scratch.

The current app is just a placeholder, and the only product source is `lib/db.js`. I’m checking the installed Next version and available cache helpers so I can use the right tag/invalidation API for this canary release instead of guessing.

text: I’ve confirmed the Next build exposes `cacheTag` and the newer `revalidateTag(tag, profile)` signature. Next I’m checking the remaining repo shape so I can implement the page cleanly and avoid introducing mismatched conventions.

I have enough context to implement this directly. I’m going to turn the home page into the catalog admin screen, add a cached product accessor tagged with `products`, and wire the sync form to an inline server action that revalidates that tag in the background-friendly way.
The page and cache path are in place. I’m building now to catch any Next/server-action type issues, especially around the inline action and cache helper import.

text:
Implemented the admin catalog page with cached product reads and a background-friendly sync trigger.

What changed:
- [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx)
  - Replaced the placeholder home page with a catalog admin UI.
  - Added an inline Server Action on a regular HTML `<form>`.
  - The action calls `revalidateTag('products', 'max')` so sync invalidation is eventual-consistency friendly instead of blocking on recomputation.
  - The page reads products through the shared cached accessor.
- [lib/products.js](/home/user/workspace/lib/products.js)
  - Wrapped the DB read in `unstable_cache(...)` with the `products` tag so catalog reads are cached instead of hitting the source on every request.
- [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx)
  - Updated metadata to match the admin catalog app.

Verification:
- `npm run build` passes.

Note:
- Next.js auto-added `".next/dev/types/**/*.ts"` to [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json) during the build.
